mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings revisions downward, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer spending momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate revenue forecasts rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or individual series ICs explain the entire predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production relative to historical trends signals expanding capacity utilization and pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, producing a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation combined with elevated policy rates forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite high inflation and rising rates, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over CPI alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly real GDP growth confirms economic expansion, which historically supports corporate profit growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth shows no statistical correlation with forward equity returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market, which sustains consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample walk-forward testing, or payroll growth alone explains all predictive variance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in housing starts reflects collapsing builder confidence and credit tightening, which precedes broader economic slowdown and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines do not precede equity drawdowns, or forward returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances.
